state_description: Medium-temperature industrial heat supplied by electric boilers, advanced heat pumps and resistive process heating.
cost_components_summary: Annualised heat-supply equipment and non-energy operating cost excluding explicit commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Representative direct heat-supply energy inputs only. Coefficients are deliberately reduced-form and not tied to a single subsector or facility.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only. No explicit retrofit eligibility, resource competition or process-specific constraints beyond broad plausibility.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as stylised reduced-form heat-service states.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band state families are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: The achievable share remains uncertain and should be stress-tested.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Replace with subsector/process-specific service or process states (e.g., food boilers, alumina calciners, chemical steam, minerals kilns).
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Could expand to multiple industrial demand technologies or process modules in TIMES, including explicit stock turnover and retrofit/new-build splits.

state_description: Reduced-form incumbent medium-temperature industrial heat with a recalibrated national-average mix of natural gas, limited coal and existing biomass/other industrial bioenergy use.
cost_components_summary: Annualised heat-supply equipment and non-energy operating cost excluding explicit commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Calibration-oriented reduced-form incumbent heat mix. Fuel coefficients were re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to improve 2025 Australian commodity closure while retaining a plausible mixed-fuel incumbent representation.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented by electricity-supply states to avoid double counting. Biogenic CO2 from biomass combustion is excluded from CO2e unless otherwise noted.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper bound only. No explicit retrofit eligibility, resource competition or process-specific constraints beyond broad plausibility.
availability_conditions: Available in all milestone years as stylised reduced-form heat-service states.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian manufacturing fuel-mix context and planning evidence on industrial efficiency/electrification, with explicit low-confidence caveats.
derivation_method: Stylised synthesis. Temperature-band state families are chosen to preserve a fuel-switch and efficiency response in the MVP model while deferring explicit process modelling.
review_notes: Balance-ready incumbent. Use as a calibration-oriented national average, not a plant-specific archetype.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Replace with subsector/process-specific service or process states (e.g., food boilers, alumina calciners, chemical steam, minerals kilns).
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Could expand to multiple industrial demand technologies or process modules in TIMES, including explicit stock turnover and retrofit/new-build splits.
balance_tuning_note: Fuel mix re-tuned in the balance-ready pack to reduce 2025 coal overstatement and improve national commodity closure. Treat as a calibration-oriented incumbent rather than a literal subsector average.
